reeval.extended_record: Comorbidity review is notable for alcohol-related hepatic steatosis confirmed on prior ultrasound, with no known HIV, no diabetes mellitus, and no active infection on this admission; renal function is preserved with creatinine 0.9 mg/dL and eGFR 92 mL/min/1.73m2, and he is not on any immunosuppressive therapy. He denies any prior pancreatic or biliary surgery, and has never undergone ERCP or other endoscopic pancreaticobiliary intervention. Contrast-enhanced CT abdomen performed on hospital day 2 shows diffuse pancreatic enlargement with peripancreatic fat stranding and a 3.2 cm nonenhancing area in the pancreatic body consistent with necrosis, without pseudocyst formation; CT severity index is 6. MRCP shows a nondilated common bile duct measuring 4 mm with no choledocholithiasis, no ductal stricture, and no mass lesion suspicious for malignancy. The main pancreatic duct is mildly irregular but patent, with no evidence of obstructing calculus or ductal disruption.

reeval.extended_record: Laboratory results from her diagnostic workup (obtained 2026-04-15) show a suppressed TSH of <0.01 mIU/L, elevated free T4 of 3.6 ng/dL (reference 0.8-1.8), and elevated free T3 of 8.9 pg/mL (reference 2.3-4.2). TSH receptor antibody (TRAb) is positive at 7.8 IU/L (reference <1.75), and thyroid-stimulating immunoglobulin (TSI) is elevated at 260% of baseline (reference <140%), confirming Graves disease. CBC shows mild normocytic anemia with hemoglobin 11.2 g/dL and a normal white cell count of 6.8 x10^9/L. Liver panel reveals mildly elevated ALT of 52 U/L and AST of 48 U/L, consistent with thyrotoxicosis-related hepatic stress; renal panel is normal with creatinine 0.7 mg/dL. She has no history of cancer, cardiac disease, hypertension, neurological conditions, or chronic fatigue syndrome. She was diagnosed with Graves disease on 2026-04-15 and started methimazole 10 mg twice daily one week later; she has had no prior antithyroid treatments, no radioactive iodine therapy, and no thyroid or other surgeries.

reeval.extended_record: Guardian provided written informed consent for research participation on 2026-07-05 and the family is being referred for screening in the NEPTUNE nephrotic syndrome registry; formal enrollment is pending confirmation and the child is not currently enrolled in any other trial. Given the boy's age, classic steroid-sensitive presentation, normal blood pressure, and absence of hematuria or renal insufficiency (creatinine 0.4 mg/dL), a renal biopsy was not performed and no genetic panel was sent; the presumptive diagnosis is minimal change disease (MCD), pending response to an 8-week course of oral prednisolone 2 mg/kg/day. Secondary-cause workup was completed and unremarkable: ASO titer and streptozyme panel negative, C3 and C4 complement within normal limits, ANA negative, hepatitis B surface antigen and hepatitis C antibody negative, HIV negative, urine culture negative, and the child is afebrile with no signs of active infection or peritonitis.

reeval.extended_record: Nasal endoscopy on hospital day 1 obtained biopsy of the necrotic turbinate; histopathology showed broad, ribbon-like, non-septate hyphae with right-angle branching, and tissue culture grew Rhizopus arrhizus at 72 hours, with Mucorales PCR positive on the same specimen, meeting EORTC-MSGERC criteria for proven invasive mucormycosis given his diabetic host factor and sino-orbital clinical criteria. Contrast CT chest showed no nodules, cavitation, or consolidation, excluding pulmonary involvement, and two sets of blood cultures plus sputum culture remained negative at 48 hours, excluding bacterial co-infection; HIV testing was negative and absolute neutrophil count was normal. The patient was alert and fully oriented, scored 29/30 on Mini-Mental State Examination, and demonstrated intact decision-making capacity, personally signing informed consent without need for a legal guardian. He carries employer-sponsored private health insurance with prior authorization approved for antifungal therapy, was admitted to Lakeshore Regional Medical Center on 2026-06-20, underwent emergent endoscopic sinus debridement on hospital day 2, and started intravenous liposomal amphotericin B; he denied any prior head or neck radiotherapy.

reeval.extended_record: HRCT demonstrates definite UIP pattern with subpleural, basal-predominant reticulation, honeycombing, and traction bronchiectasis without atypical features. Serologic workup is negative: RF <10 IU/mL, anti-CCP <5 U/mL, ANA negative, anti-Scl-70 negative, anti-Jo-1 negative, confirming absence of connective tissue disease. Multidisciplinary discussion (pulmonology, radiology, pathology) on 2026-05-14 confirmed a diagnosis of idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis, excluding RA-ILD, hypersensitivity pneumonitis, and other secondary causes. The patient is not in acute exacerbation; her dyspnea has been slowly progressive over 8 months without recent acute worsening. Room air SpO2 is 93%, with exertional desaturation to 87% on 6-minute walk test; she does not require supplemental oxygen or ventilatory support at rest. Recent chest CT and clinical assessment show no new ground-glass opacities, procalcitonin is <0.05 ng/mL, BNP is 42 pg/mL, and CT pulmonary angiogram is negative, excluding infection, heart failure, and pulmonary embolism. She was started on nintedanib 150 mg twice daily six weeks ago, tolerating it with mild diarrhea; no corticosteroids, immunosuppressants, or known drug hypersensitivities.

reeval.extended_record: The patient denies any history of autoimmune disease, immunodeficiency, organ failure, or psychiatric illness, and takes no chronic medications. He signed informed consent for trial participation on 2026-07-10, witnessed by the study coordinator. Serologic testing shows VCA-IgM positive at 1:320, VCA-IgG positive at 1:640, and EBNA-IgG negative, a pattern consistent with acute primary EBV infection. Quantitative EBV DNA viral load by PCR is 45,000 copies/mL in whole blood. CBC reveals lymphocytosis with 55% atypical lymphocytes and mild thrombocytopenia (platelets 130,000/uL). Liver function tests show mild transaminitis (ALT 85 U/L, AST 78 U/L). Renal function is normal (creatinine 0.9 mg/dL). Abdominal ultrasound confirms splenomegaly measuring 14 cm without evidence of rupture. Vital signs are stable with temperature 38.2°C. The patient is alert and cooperative, with no psychiatric symptoms noted on mental status exam.

reeval.extended_record: Right eye findings confirm a macula-off rhegmatogenous retinal detachment; the patient first noticed the curtain-like field cut 4 days ago, with preceding photopsia and floaters beginning 6 days ago, and dilated exam localizes a single horseshoe retinal break at the 10 o'clock meridian with associated epiretinal membrane changes graded as proliferative vitreoretinopathy (PVR) grade B. Ocular surgical history for the right eye is notable for uncomplicated phacoemulsification cataract surgery with posterior chamber intraocular lens implantation performed 18 months ago; the patient is pseudophakic and has never undergone vitrectomy or scleral buckle in that eye. Given the macula-off status, pseudophakic lens, and PVR grade B findings, the surgical team has scheduled a standard 25-gauge pars plana vitrectomy (PPV) with intraoperative laser retinopexy and 20% SF6 gas tamponade for the right eye, proceeding on an urgent basis within 24 hours rather than as an elective outpatient case.
